zh.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot)於1175年與De FRISE結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot)于1175年与De FRISE结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cayeux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Landry, Alphonse, Andre, Armand, Colette, Monique, Theophile.
French (northern): from an old form of   Mayeur  . Compare   Mayeaux  .

variants.name: Mayeur
meanings: French:
(northern): variant of   Maire  .
(Lorraine): variant of   Mayer   (see   Meyer   1).

variants.name: Mayeaux
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Clemile, Ferrel.
Altered form of French   Mayeux  .

nameMeanings.name: (dit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Indian and Bangladeshi: variant of   Deep  .

variants.name: Deep
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Akash, Prem, Vishal.
Indian (northern states) and Bangladeshi: from Hindi dīp ‘lamp’, Sanskrit dīpa. It occurs commonly as the final element of compound personal names, e.g. in Kuldeep ‘light of the family’.
English: habitational name from Dieppe in Pas-de-Calais.

nameMeanings.name: "Caillot)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French:

from Old French maillot ‘big mallet’, used as a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked with such an implement, e.g. a smith, and perhaps also as a nickname for a fearsome warrior (see English Mallett 2). This is the usual form of the surname in France (it is, however, most frequent in Réunion), while in North America it is normally spelled Mailhot .

variants.name: Mailloux
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Girard, Marcel, Adrien, Aime, Fernand, Herve, Michel, Normand, Pierre, Raoul, Serge, Ulderic.
French: variant of Maillou, a dialect form of Maillot ‘big mallet’.
Breton (Finistère; also Le Mailloux): unflattering nickname from mailhous ‘dirty fellow’.

variants.name: Mailhot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Adrien, Andre, Emile, Alphonse, Benoit, Fernand, Georges, Henri, Honore, Jean Claude.
French: variant of   Maillot   ‘big mallet’. This is the usual form of the surname in North America; in France it is found mainly in Puy-de-Dôme. Compare   Mayotte  .
History: René Maillot/Mailhot dit Laviolette from Castet-Arrouy in Gers, France, married Marie Chapacou in QC in 1671.

variants.name: Maillard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Pierre, Philippe, Francoise, Jean-Marie, Julien, Michel, Yves.  Irish Aileen, Fitzgerald.
French: cognate of   Maillot   ‘big mallet’.

French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Magilhard, composed of the elements magil, an augmented form of magin, magan ‘strength, might’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’, or Madelhard, from madal ‘council’ + hard. Compare Mayard .

variants.name: Myott
meanings: Altered form of French Maillot ‘big mallet’ or of its variant Mailhot , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Myatt 3.
English: variant of   Myatt   1.

variants.name: Mayotte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Mireille.
Altered form of French Maillot ‘big mallet’ or of its variant Mailhot , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Mallett
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Malot, more rarely Malet, a pet form of Mall , Mald (i.e. Maud, Matilda) + the diminutive suffix -ot or -et. In post-medieval forms of the surname final -ot was generally altered to -et(t). Though there are alternative Norman origins (see below), this is the usual source of the English surname.

English: of Norman origin, a nickname from Old French maillet ‘hammer’ (see Maillet , compare Mallet 4), used as a battle weapon. Robert Malet, lord of the Honour of Eye (Suffolk) in 1086, was the son of William Malet of Graville-Sainte-Honorine (Seine-Maritime). The latter is referred to as Gulielmo agnomine Mal(l)eto ‘William, surnamed Mal(l)et’ in the late 11th-century Gesta Guillelmi of William of Poitiers and the early 12th-century Historia Ecclesia of Orderic Vitalis. According to tradition, this surname arose because of William's reputation for bludgeoning his enemies in battle (cum) malleo ‘with the hammer’. The story may be true or it may have been invented to explain the name, in which case see 3 below for an alternative etymology.
English: of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Malet, a pet form of Malo . Compare Mallet 2.

en.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) married De FRISE in 1175. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) a épousé De FRISE en 1175. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) hat 1175 De FRISE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) lui sposato De FRISE nel 1175. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli.

ja.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot)は1175年にDe FRISEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot)(은)는 1175년 De FRISE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) casou-se com De FRISE em 1175. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) женился на женщине по имени De FRISE в 1175 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: d'Engoudessen & de Cayeux (dit "Caillot) se casó con De FRISE en 1175. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
